title: Clean-air sleep rotation in smoke or dust
summary: A worst-case routine for choosing the cleanest sleeping area and rotating watchers during smoke, dust, or ash.
content: Clean-air sleep rotation in smoke or dust is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is choosing the cleanest sleeping area and rotating watchers during smoke, dust, or ash. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.

short_term: For the first hours of Clean-air sleep rotation in smoke or dust, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Clean-air sleep rotation in smoke or dust remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Clean-air sleep rotation in smoke or dust fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
